source.deck: Hungarian FSI I Vocab and Basic Sentences
source.anki_name: Hungarian FSI I Vocab and Basic Sentences (AnkiWeb shared deck)

rows.english: What's the station like? Is it big?
Which is the way to the airport?
What's the airport like? Is it big?
There's a restaurant to the right.
There's a café to the left.
What would you like to have?
I ask, I want, I beg
Oh, yes. I also want some cigarettes.
Here you are. The price is five fillérs.
What would you like to have?
What kind of bread do you want?

rows.hungarian: A kávé és a tea is jó.
rows.english: The coffee and the tea are very good also.
I beg your pardon, excuse me
Excuse me, what time is it?
you leave, start, depart; he, she, it leaves. starts, departs
you arrive; he, she, it arrives
At what time does the train arrive?
At what time does the movie begin?
How much is two and three?
How much is five and six?
How much is four and eight?
How much is five and five?

rows.hungarian: Rózsa János és Johnsonék találkoznak Budapesten.
rows.english: John Rose and the Johnsons meet in Budapest.
Peter Johnson is a Foreign Service Officer.

rows.hungarian: Jó napot, Johnson úr! Hogy van?
rows.english: Hello, Mr. Johnson! How are you?
I haven't seen you for a long time.
you hurry; he, she, it hurries
It's been a long time, indeed.
What do you intend to buy?
I intend to buy a hat.
you want (speaking to family member or intimate friend)
What kind of a hat do you want to buy, Mary?
How do you like Budapest, Madam?
But I'm in a hurry now.
you do, make; he, she, it does, makes

rows.hungarian: Maga most mit csinál. Johnson úr?
rows.english: What are you going to do now, Mr. Johnson?
you come; he, she, it comes
Thank you, I'll be glad to.
to take a seat, to sit down
you bring; he, she, it brings

rows.hungarian: A pincér mindjárt hoz egy pohár vizet. Éhes?
rows.english: The waiter will bring a glass of water right away. Are you hungry?

rows.hungarian: Mondja, ki az a magas hölgy?
rows.english: Say, who is that tall lady?
She's Mary Taylor. Don't you know her?
No, I don't. Is she an American?
No, she's not. she's a Hungarian.
I often speak Hungarian with her.
But she speaks English well, too.
What will you be doing in Vienna?

rows.hungarian: Vásárolunk. A feleségem Budapesten nem talál semmit.
rows.english: We're going shopping. My wife can't find anything in Budapest,
What does she want to buy?

rows.hungarian: De itt nem talál pirosat, csak sárgát.
rows.english: But here she can't find ahy red ones, only yellow ones.
You'll surely find everything in Vienna.

rows.hungarian: Biztosan. És ha szép táskát találok nem egyet hozok, hanem kettőt.
rows.english: Of course. And if I find any nice bags, I'll bring not one but two.
Don't you intend to buy anything else?

rows.hungarian: De igen. Egy pár cipőt is akarok venni.
rows.english: yes, I intend to buy a pair of shoes also.
you remain, stay; he, she, it remains, stays
How long are you staying there?
Just for a couple of weeks.
Then I intend to go back to Budapest.
Thank you. Have a nice trip.
you go; he, she, it goes

rows.hungarian: Molnár úr vásárolni megy Johnson úrral.
rows.english: Mr. Miller is going shopping with Mr. Johnson.
I'd like to buy a new suit.
Do you know a good store?

rows.hungarian: De tegnap vettem egy jó ruhát az Állami Áruházban.
rows.english: But yesterday I bought a good suit at the State store.
They have some fairly good men's suits there.
How much do you want to spend?
I don't want to spend a lot.
How much does a good suit cost?
Isn't it possible to get a cheaper one?

rows.hungarian: A Petőfi Sándor utcában van egy kis üzlet.
rows.english: There's a small shop on Alexander Petőfi street.
Perhaps we'll get a cheaper one there.
I'd like a light-weight summer suit.

rows.hungarian: Világosbarnát vagy szürkét, de nem nagyon drágát.
rows.english: Light brown or grey, but not very expensive.

rows.hungarian: Az a szürke ruha a kirakatban nagyon tetszik.
rows.english: I like that grey suit in the show. window a lot.
is becoming to you, suits you
This looks very good on you.

rows.hungarian: Köszönöm, az nem kell, de néhány inget szeretnék.
rows.english: No, thank you, I don't need any, but I would like a few shirts.
What kind of shirts do you want?
We received many nice ones yesterday.
Do you have blue ones also?
to know, to know how, to be able
In America it's fifteen and a half.

rows.hungarian: Azt hiszem, ez elég nagy lesz.
rows.english: I think this one will be big enough.

rows.hungarian: Egy kéket és két fehéret kérek.
rows.english: I want a blue one and two white ones.
Thanks, I don't want neckties now.
Is there a perfumery here sornewhere?

rows.hungarian: Jobbra, a harmadik házban van egy.
rows.english: There's one in the third building to the right.
Good morning! I'd like some soap.
How many bars do you want?
Do you have shaving soap also?
Yes, we have. It also is 'quality'.
Do you want some toothpaste also?
Yes, I do want some also.
Do you want a small or a large tube?
I'm sorry, but we're out of razor blades.
No, thank you. How much is the bill?

rows.hungarian: Mondja kérem, hol tudunk edényt venni?
rows.english: Tell me please, where can we buy dishes?

rows.hungarian: A Kossuth Lajos utcában van egy edénybolt.
rows.english: There's a china shop on Lajos Kossuth street.
They have very beautiful dishes there.

rows.hungarian: Itt vagyunk a Kossuth Lajos utcában.
rows.english: Here we are on Louis Kossuth street.

rows.hungarian: Milyen szép edények vannak a kirakatban!
rows.english: What beautiful dishes there are in the show window!
Of course we have cheap plates also.

rows.hungarian: Mennyibe kerül ez a hat tányér?
rows.english: How much do these six plates cost?

rows.hungarian: És mennyibe kerülnek azok a kékek?
rows.english: And how much do those blue ones cost?
I'd like a few glasses also.
What kind of glasses do you have?
I want to buy only plain glasses.
These are beautiful and not expensive.

rows.hungarian: És kérem, csomagolja be azokat is.

rows.english: I forgot to buy some aspirin and cigarettes.
You can get medicine only in a pharmacy.
Cigarettes, however, you get at the tobacco shop.
Is it possible to get American cigarettes?
It's difficult to get them in Pest.
We usually get them in Vienna.
Next week I'm going to Vienna.
Then I'll bring some for you too. O.K.?
I'll be very grateful if you do.
I want a box of aspirin.
I'd like some bandage and antiseptic.
Do you want wide or narrow bandage?
We have plenty of wide bandage at home.

rows.hungarian: És kérek egy kis üveg fertőtlenítőt.
rows.english: And I want a small bottle of antiseptic.
Here you are. That's 70 forints altogether.
Are you still going to buy something more?
I'm still going to buy two pairs of children's stockings.

rows.hungarian: A kislány már tizenkét éves elmúlt.

rows.english: Where do they go to school?

rows.hungarian: A fiú az angol elemibe jár, a kislány meg középiskolába.
rows.english: The boy goes to the English grarnmar school, and the girl to high school.
Do they like to go to school?

rows.hungarian: Igen. Mind a ketté jó tanuló.

rows.english: What's the summer like in Hungary?

rows.hungarian: Igen. Budán lakunk egy nagy lakásban.
rows.english: Yes. We live in a big apartment in Buda.

rows.hungarian: Már be van a lakás rendezve?
Egy rádiót és néhány lámpát kell venni.
rows.english: We have to buy a radio and a few lamps.
I hope you find niee lamps.
But I've got to go now. See you later.

rows.hungarian: Feketéék vacsorára hívtak egy amerikai házaspárt, Johnsonekat.
rows.english: The Blacks have invited an American couple, the Johnsons, for supper.
you carry, take; he, she, it carries, takes
the lady of the house, landlady
to the lady of the house

rows.hungarian: Johnson úr virágot visz a háziasszonynak.
rows.english: Mr. Johnson brings flowers for the hostess.
Let me introduce my wife, Eve.
I don't believe you've met yet.
My husband often speaks about you.

rows.hungarian: Mindenhol fák és kertek vannak a városban.
rows.english: There are trees and gardens everywhere in the city.

rows.hungarian: Sok szép virág van minden kertben.
rows.english: And so many beautiful flowers in every garden!

rows.hungarian: Igen, a város nagyon szép tavasszal.
rows.english: Yes, the city is very beautiful in the springtime.

rows.hungarian: Nemcsak tavasszal szép, hanem ősszel is.
rows.english: It's beautiful not only in spring but in the fall also.

rows.hungarian: Sok hegy és erdő van a környéken.
rows.english: There are many hills and forests in the vicinity.
to make, to go on an excursion
Do you often go hiking in the mountains?

rows.hungarian: Igen. Vasárnap mindig felmegyünk a hegyekbe.
rows.english: Yes. On Sundays we always go up into the mountains.

rows.hungarian: A gyerekek szeretnek halászni a patakban.
rows.english: The children like to fish in the creek.

rows.hungarian: Egy kis sört vagy konyakot parancsolnak?
rows.english: Would you like some beer or cognac?

rows.hungarian: Köszönöm szépen, én nem iszom semmit.
rows.english: Thank you very much, but I don't drink anything.
What's the latest in the United States?
there isn't; you aren't; he, she, it isn't

rows.hungarian: Milyen szép nagy ez a lakás!
rows.english: What a lovely, big apartment this is!

rows.hungarian: De nem szeretek ott lakni, mert a konyha nem modern.
rows.english: But I don't like to live there, because the kitchen isn't modern.

rows.hungarian: Ebben az utcában van egy ház kiadó.
rows.english: There's a house for rent on this street.

rows.hungarian: A ház mögött nagy kert van.
rows.english: Behind the house is a big garden.
How many rooms are there in the house?

rows.hungarian: egy ebédlő, egy nappali, egy dohányzó, harom halószoba és természetesen konyha és fürdőszoba.
rows.english: a dining room, a living room, a study, three bedrooms, and, of course, a kitchen and a bathroom.
I'm going to look at it tomorrow.
But now let's go into the dining room.

rows.hungarian: Milyen gyönyörű rózsák vannak a vázában!
rows.english: What beautiful roses there are in the vase!

rows.hungarian: Mindennap friss virágot teszek a vázákba.
rows.english: I put fresh flowers in the vases every day.
you play; he, she plays the piano

rows.hungarian: Egy fekete zongorát láttam a másik szobában.
rows.english: I saw a black piano in the other room.
Does she play modern or classical music?

rows.hungarian: De van egy lemezjátszó és néhány modern lemez a másik szobában.
rows.english: But we have a phonograph and some modern records in the other room.

rows.hungarian: Nagyon szeretem a magyar népdalokat és a cigányzenét.
rows.english: I like Hungarian folksongs and gypsy music very much.

rows.hungarian: De szívesen játszom néhány magyar dalt a zongorán.
rows.english: But I'll be happy to play some Hungarian songs on the piano.
Let's go out into the garden.
The view from there is lovely.

rows.hungarian: Ott eszünk egy kis fagylaltot és iszunk valamit.
rows.english: There we'll have some ice cream and something to drink.

rows.hungarian: Köszonom, én nem eszem sohasem fagylaltot.
rows.english: Thank you, I never eat ice cream.
Will you have a little ice cream?

rows.hungarian: Parancsol egy kis barackpálinkát, Johnson úr?
rows.english: Would you like some apricot brandy, Mr. Johnson?

rows.hungarian: Nagyon késó van, már fél tizenkettő.
rows.english: It's very late, half past eleven already.
I'm afraid we'll have to go home.

rows.hungarian: Nagyon köszönjük ezt a kellemes estét.
rows.english: Thank you very much for the delightful evening.

rows.hungarian: Remélem, hogy maguk is nemsokára meglátogatnak minket.
rows.english: I hope that you also will come to visit us soon.

rows.hungarian: Először menjünk a Magyar Nemzeti Bankba.
rows.english: Let's first go to the Hungarian National Bank.
I want to cash a check.
We're going to see the city.
What branch office do you wanr to go to?

rows.hungarian: Úgy tudom, a városháza mellett van egy.
rows.english: I think there's one next to the city hall.

rows.hungarian: Tudja, hogy hol van a városháza?
rows.english: Do you know where the city hall is?
I don't know - no use asking me.

rows.hungarian: Miért nem kérdezi meg a portást?
rows.english: Why don't you ask the doorman?

rows.hungarian: Mondja kérem, hol van a városháza?
rows.english: Will you tell me, please, where the city hall is?

rows.hungarian: Menjen be autóbuszon vagy villamoson a belvárosba.
rows.english: Go downtown by bus or by streetcar.
It's just in front of the door.

rows.hungarian: A villamosmegálló pedig a park mellett van.
rows.english: However, the streetcar stop is right next to the park.
Where do I have to get off?

rows.hungarian: A jegyszedő majd megmondja, hol kell leszállni.
rows.english: The driver will tell you later where you have to get off.

rows.hungarian: A Nemzeti Bank mellett van, a Szabadság téren.
rows.english: It's next to the National Bank, on Liberty Square.

rows.hungarian: Ma szombat van, így nem dolgozunk.
rows.english: It's saturday today, and so we're not working.
After the bank let's go for a walk.
I'd like to see the Museum of Fine Arts.
All right, let's visit the picture gallery.
What kind of paintings do you like? The modern ones?

rows.hungarian: Nem a modern képeket szeretem, hanem a régieket.
rows.english: I don't like modern paintings but old ones.
Where's the Museum of Fine Arts?

rows.hungarian: Ott volt közel a híres Sztálin-szobor is.
rows.english: The famaus Stalin monument used to be near there also.
Isn't your wife coming with us?
She's cooking the dinner in the morning.

rows.hungarian: Azután elviszi a gyerekeket az állatkertbe.
rows.english: Afterwards she's taking the children to the zoo.
In the afternoon she's taking them to a pastry shop.

rows.hungarian: A gyerekek nagyon szeretnek fagylaltot enni.
rows.english: The children like to eat ice cream very much.
I'm not acquainted with the city yet.
What's there in Budapest worth looking at?

rows.hungarian: A múzeum után megmutatom a várat is.
rows.english: After the museum I''ll show you the fort also.

rows.hungarian: Ott van a hires Mátyás -templom és a Halászbástya.
rows.english: The famous Matthias Church and the Fishermen's Bastion are there.

rows.hungarian: A Bazilika is a Várban van?
rows.english: Is the cathedral also in the fort?

rows.hungarian: Nem. A vár Budán van, a Bazilika pedig Pesten.
rows.english: No. The fort is in Buda; the cathedral, however, is in Pest.
Yes, it's on the bank of the Danube.
(Late afternoon in a café on the bank of the Danube)
you swim; he, she, it swims, floats, sails

rows.hungarian: Mindig ilyen sok hajó úszik a Dunán?
rows.english: Are there always so many ships on the Danube?

rows.hungarian: Igen. Sok a teherszállító és a személyszállító hajó.
rows.english: Yes. There are many freighters and passenger ships.
There are, of course, excursion boats also.

rows.hungarian: A tehnerszállító hajók hoszák a gyümölesöt és a zöldséget a fővárosba.
rows.english: The freighters brinq frult and vagetables into the capital.

rows.hungarian: Innen pedig a gépeket viszik más városokba es falukba.
rows.english: From here, on the other hand, they carry machines into other cities and villages.
Is it possible to swim in the Danube?

rows.hungarian: Nyáron lehet, de a víz hideg.
rows.english: It's possible in the summer, but the water is cold.
There are many baths in Budapest.

rows.hungarian: A beteg emberek főleg gyógyfürdőkbe járnak.
rows.english: Sick people in particular frequent the thermal baths.
How many people there are on the bank of the Danube!

rows.hungarian: Ha nem fáradt, mi is menjünk sétálni. Jó?
rows.english: If you aren't tired, let's go for a walk also, shall we?

rows.hungarian: Szívesen, csak először megiszom a kávémat.
rows.english: Gladly, only I'll finish my coffee first.
Aren't you going to eat your pastry?
I'll call the walter right away and pay hlm.

rows.hungarian: Éppen most gyújtják meg a lámpákat a Dunaparton.
rows.english: Right now they're lighting the lamps on the bank of the Danube.
Do you see them? They're already on.

rows.hungarian: Gyönyörű! De itt jön a pincér.
rows.english: Magnificent! But here comes the waiter.

rows.hungarian: Kisék és Nagyék egy bérházban laknak a Bajza utcaban.
rows.english: The Kises and the Nagys live in an apartment house on Bajza street.
I'd like to borrow a typewriter.

rows.hungarian: Néhánr levelet akarok írni, de az írogépem a hivatalban van, a töltőtollam pedig rossz.
rows.english: I want to write a few letters, but my typewriter is in the office and my fountain pen is broken.

rows.hungarian: Sajnos, az én írógépem sincs itthon.
rows.english: Unfortunately my typewriter isn't here either.

rows.hungarian: De itt van a nővérem írógépe.

rows.english: She'll be glad to lend it to you.
you work: he, she, it works
She works in the National Bank.
She's the secretary to one of the directors.
Yes. Her director is a fine man.

rows.hungarian: Köszönöm, csak egy percre, mert késő van.
rows.english: Thanks, just for a minute, because it's late.
I still want to go to the post office.
Don't you want something from there?

rows.hungarian: Ha hoz tíz levelezőlapot és öt légipostalevelet, megköszönöm.
rows.english: If you bring me ten post cards and five aerogrammes, I'll be grateful

rows.hungarian: Látom, hogy itt van a Béke és Szabadság utolsó száma.
rows.english: I see that you have the last issue of Béke és Szabadság.
to take, to subscribe to a paper

rows.hungarian: A lányom az Ország Világot járatja.

rows.english: Does your wife like to read also?

rows.hungarian: Igen. ő főleg regényeket és verseket olvas.
rows.english: Yes. She reads novels and poems mostly.

rows.hungarian: De szereti a külföldi írókat is.
rows.english: But she likes foreign writers also.

rows.hungarian: Petőfi az én kedvenc költőm is.
rows.english: Petőfi is my favorite poet too.
Can you recommend a good book?
Do you like the new Hungarian writers?

rows.hungarian: Nagyon! Sajnos, sok jó írónk hallgat mostanában.
rows.english: Very much. Unfortunately many of our good writers remain silent these days.

rows.hungarian: Úgy hallom, nem akarnak a kommunista rendszernek írni.
rows.english: I hear that they don't want to write for the Communist regime.

rows.hungarian: Megértem. De itt van egy érdekes könyv.
rows.english: I can understand that. But here's an interesting book.
I recommend it. Will you take it?
I'll bring it back next week.

rows.hungarian: Mondja, kedves barátom hogy van az édesapja?
rows.english: Tell me, my dear friend, how's your (dear) father?

rows.hungarian: Az édesapám már elég öreg de hála Isten jól van.
rows.english: My father is already quite old, but thank goodness he's weIl.
What does he do all day?

rows.hungarian: A házuk mögött van egy kis kert.
rows.english: There's a small garden behind their house.

rows.hungarian: Ha jó idő van ott ül és gyönyörködik a kertjükben.
rows.english: If the weather is good he sits there and enjoys their garden.

rows.hungarian: Az ő házuk Pest-környékén van, a Dunaparton.
rows.english: Their house is in the outskirts of Pest on the bank of the Danube.
to take care of, look after
She takes care of my father.

rows.hungarian: Az egyik fivérem is ott lakik.
rows.english: One of my brothers lives there too.
The one who was in Moscow?

rows.hungarian: Igen, a bátyám két évig Moszkvában volt.
rows.english: Yes, my older brother was in Moscow for two years.
He's now teaching in one of the universities.

rows.hungarian: És mit csinál a másik fivére?
rows.english: And what is your other brother doing?
He's just buying a German car.
Is your brother buying a new car?
Then he certainly is a rich man.
to make good money, earn a good living

rows.hungarian: Nem éppen gazdag, de jól keres.
rows.english: He isn't exactly rich, but he's earning a lot of money.
you have a nice apartment, Mr. Nagy.

rows.hungarian: Tíz év óta lakunk itt és még a regi lakbért fizetjük.
rows.english: We have been living here for ten years and we still pay the old rent.

rows.hungarian: Sajnos, az én lakásom nagyon drága, pedig a hálószobánk kicsi.
rows.english: Unfortunately my apartment is very expensive, although our bedroom is small.
I'd like to buy a house.

rows.hungarian: Azt hiszem, az egyik barátom telefonál.
rows.english: I believe one of my friends is on the phone.
I'll have to be leaving now, anyway.
I will bring it back this evening. All right?
How long have you beeq here in Hungary, Consul?
I've been here for three months.
Is your family also in Budapest?
Are they coming from America by ship or plane?
They're flying from New York to Vienna.
How are you going to Vienna? By train?
Either by train or by car.

rows.hungarian: Képzelem mennyire örül, hogy jön a családja.
rows.english: I can imagine how happy you are that your family's coming.

rows.hungarian: Igen, nagyon boldog vagyok, hogy vegre itt lesznek.
rows.english: Yes, I'm very happy that they'll be here at last.

rows.hungarian: Oroszországban volt, de most Szegeden tanul az egyetemen.
rows.english: He was in Russia, but he's studying now at the University of Szeged.

rows.hungarian: A kedves lánya is volt már külföldön?
rows.english: Has your daughter also already been abroad?
Yes, she's been abroad many times.

rows.hungarian: Mint sportoló gyakran megy más országokba.
rows.english: As a sportswoman she goes often to other countries.

rows.hungarian: Volt már Kelet-Németországban, Svédországban és Olaszországban is.
rows.english: She has already been in East Germany, Sweden and Italy also.

rows.hungarian: A jövő héten jön haza Franciaországból.
rows.english: She's coming home next week from France.
I suppose your daughter plays tennis?

rows.hungarian: Majdnem minden nemzetközi versenyen részt vesz.
rows.english: She takes part in nearly every international tournament.

rows.hungarian: Igen. A férje a külügyminisztériumban dolgozik.
rows.english: Yes. Her husband works in the Ministry of Foreign Affairs.
Then I'll surely meet them sometime.

rows.hungarian: Mondja, ki az az úr, aki most jön ki a másik szobából?
rows.english: Tell me, who's the gentleman coming out of the other room now?

rows.hungarian: Úgy tudom, hogy hétfőn érkezik Svédországból.
rows.english: I believe she's arriving from Sweden on Monday.

rows.hungarian: És kik azok, akik most beszélgetnek vele?
rows.english: And who are those people chatting with him now?

rows.hungarian: Az a francia követ és a felesége.
rows.english: That's the French Minister and his wife.

rows.hungarian: És az a szép asszony, aki az attasé mellett áll?
rows.english: And that beautiful woman standing next to the attaché?
That's the wife of the Italian chargé d'Affaires.
Are there only Western diplomats here?

rows.hungarian: Óh, nem. Látja azokat az urakat a sarokban?
rows.english: Oh, no. Do you see those gentlemen in the corner?

rows.hungarian: Az egyik az orosz sajtóattasé, a masik egy lengyel újságíró.
rows.english: One of them is the Russian press attaché; the other is a Polish journalist.

rows.hungarian: Egy török követségi titkar beszélget velük.
rows.english: A Turkish Legation secretary's chatting with them.
I believe they're drinking Tokay wine.
Shall we not have some also?
There's some there on the table.
I see many people are leaving already.
It's only five minutes past ten.
At what time do you want to leave?
I'll be going at half past ten.

rows.hungarian: Látja azt az urat, aki most búcsúzik a háziasszonytól?
rows.english: Do you see that gentleman now saying goodby to the hostess?
He's the Minister of Foreign Affairs.
Are there many here from the Ministry of Foreign Affairs?

rows.hungarian: A politikai osztály főnöke van itt és egy vagy két osztályvezető.
rows.english: The chief of the political section and one or two department heads are here.

rows.hungarian: A külügyminiszter helyettese is itt van?
rows.english: Is the Under Secretary of state also here?
What are you going to do this summer?
to spend the summer holidays, pass the summer
a summer resort on the Balaton

rows.hungarian: A Balatonon akarunk nyaralni az idén.
rows.english: We want to spend our summer vacation at the Balaton this year.
Have you already been at the Balaton?
to enjoy, have a good time
Of course! We always have a good time there.

rows.hungarian: A gyerekek is nagyon szeretik a Balatont.
rows.english: The children also like the Balaton very much.

rows.hungarian: Azon az oldalon a víz nem mély.
rows.english: On that side the water isnlt deep.
What do you do all day long?
The children play in the water.

rows.hungarian: A feleségem úszik vagy csónakázik, én pedig fekszem a fák alatt és alszom.
rows.english: My wife swims or goes boat riding; however, I lie under the trees and sleep.
When do you plan to go?
We'll go in June, the end of the month.
Then it will (already) be warm enough.
The Tóths live in the Alföld.
Isn't that Tóth standiryg at the gate?
Have you had a nice trip?

rows.hungarian: Nem rossz, csak nem tudtam gyorsan hajtani.
rows.english: Not bad, only I wasn't able to drive fast.

rows.hungarian: Sok szekér, teherautó és traktor volt az úton.
rows.english: There were many carts, trucks and tractors on the road.

rows.hungarian: Szép kis gazdasága van, Tóth úr!
rows.english: You have a beautiful small farm, Mr. Tóth.

rows.hungarian: Elég szép, de sajnos túl kicsi.
rows.english: It's nice enough, but unfortunately too small.
Are you also a member of the coöperative?

rows.hungarian: Igen, ebben a faluban mindenki tagja.
rows.english: Yes, everybody in this village is a member.

rows.hungarian: Hány hold földje van a szövetkezetnek?
rows.english: How many acres of land does the coöperative have?
We grow crops on it for the most part.

rows.hungarian: Ott tartják a szövetkezet lovait és teheneit.
rows.english: That's where they keep the horses and the cows belonging to the coöperative.

rows.hungarian: Vannak saját teheneik az embereknek Magyarországon?
rows.english: Do people have their own cows in Hungary?

rows.hungarian: Minden családnak lehet egy vagy két saját tehene.
rows.english: Every family may own one or two cows.
Can they keep their own poultry also?

rows.hungarian: Igen, a ház körül lehet tartani.
rows.english: Yes, they can keep them around the house.
What do you do with them?
We eat them or sell them at the market.
I see you have a nice orchard.
What kind of fruit trees do you have?
I have apple and apricot trees for the most part.

rows.hungarian: De van egy-két körte- és cseresznyefám is.
rows.english: But I also have a few pear and cherry trees.

rows.hungarian: Azt hiszem, jó termésünk lesz az idén.
rows.english: I think we'll have a good crop this year.
No, they're my neighbor's raspberry shrubs.

rows.hungarian: Szép nagy háza van a szomszérljának!
rows.english: Your neighbor has a beautifui big house!

rows.hungarian: A ház ablakai is milyen nagyok!
rows.english: And how large the windows of the house are!

rows.hungarian: Igen, szép. A háború előtt gazdálkodó volt.
rows.english: Yes, it's nice. Before the war he was a farmer.

rows.hungarian: Kinek a kutyái ugatnak az úton?
rows.english: Whose dogs are those barking on the road?

rows.hungarian: Nem, azok nem a mi kutyáink.
Azok a szomszédom fiának a kutyái.
Nekünk csak egy kutyánk van, egy házőrző kutya.
rows.english: We have only one dog, a watch dog.
He's lying there in front of the gate.

rows.hungarian: Az a maguk kertje a ház mögött?
rows.english: Is that your garden behind the house?
Yes, that's my wife's kitchen garden.

rows.hungarian: Mindenféle zöldség van a kertben, főleg paradicsom.
rows.english: There are all sorts of vegetables in the garden, tomatoes in particular.

rows.hungarian: Eper is van a kert egyik sarkában.
rows.english: There are also strawberries in one corner of the garden.

rows.hungarian: A felesége egyedül öntözi a kertet?
rows.english: Does your wife water the garden alone?

rows.hungarian: Szeretnek, de itthon is sokat dolgoznak.
rows.english: Yes they do, but they work hard at home also.

rows.hungarian: A feleségem ruháit is ők varrják.
rows.english: They sew my wife's dresses also.
You have clever daughters. Mr. Tóth!
They work in the State Farm.
Do they get a good salary?

rows.hungarian: Milyen szép tolluk van azoknak a madaraknak!
rows.english: What beautiful feathers those birds have!

rows.hungarian: A szomszédom azt mondja, hogy ezek az ő madarai, és csak neki énekelnek!
rows.english: My neighbor says that these are his birds and they sing only for him!

rows.hungarian: Azt hiszem, a felesége hívja magát, Tóth úr.
rows.english: I think your wife is calling you. Mr. Tóth.

rows.hungarian: Paprikás csirke és almás rétes lesz ebédre.
rows.english: We'll have chicken paprika and apple strudel for dinner.
I want to go to Siófok.

rows.hungarian: Érdeklődni szeretnék, hogyan lehet Siófokra utazni.
rows.english: I'd like to inquire about how one can travel to Siófok.
Do you want to go by train or by bus?

rows.hungarian: Reggel kilenckor indul egy gyorsvonat a Keleti pályaudvarról.
rows.english: There's an express train that starts at nine in the morning from the East Station.

rows.hungarian: Van egy személyvonat is, ami nyolckor indul,
rows.english: There's a local train also which starts at eight,

rows.hungarian: de az csak délben érkezik Siófokra.
rows.english: but doesn't arrive in Siófok before noon.
Then I'll go on the express.
Can I get a ticket here?
railway carriage with wooden benches (third class)
A cushioned seat or a hard one?

rows.hungarian: A párnás a régi első vagy második osztály, ott az ülés puha.
rows.english: The cushioned is the old first or second class; there the seat is soft.

rows.hungarian: A fapados a régi harmadik osztály, ahol az emberek fapadokon ülnek.
rows.english: The 'fapados' is the old third class, where people sit on wooden benches.

rows.hungarian: Akkor kérek két jegyet a párnásra.

rows.english: Here you are. I'd like to ask something else.

rows.hungarian: Hogy jutunk ki reggel az állomásra?
rows.english: How can we get out to the station in the morning?

rows.hungarian: A Szabadság téren van az amerikai követség.
rows.english: The American Legation is on Szabadság Square.

rows.hungarian: Az épülettől balra van az autóbuszmegálló.
rows.english: The bus stop is to the left of the building.

rows.hungarian: Az a Keleti pályaudvar bejárata előtt áll meg.
rows.english: It stops in front of the entrance to the East Station.
Thank you very much for the information.
Don't mention it. Have a pleasant trip.

rows.hungarian: Ez a két bőrönd a mienk.

rows.english: We're going first class to Siófok. on the express.
On which track is the train?
Fourth track. on the right side.
Is it possible to get on board yet?
after, to, till (with reference to time)

rows.hungarian: Negyed óra múlva lehet csak beszállni.
rows.english: You can get on only after 15 minutes.

rows.hungarian: De én felteszem a csomagokat most a vonatra.
rows.english: But I'lI put the luggage into the train now.
Can I still buy a newspaper?

rows.hungarian: Igen. Az újságárus a kijárat mellett van.
rows.english: Yes. The newsdealer is next to the exit.

rows.hungarian: Jó napot, Horváth úr. Hova utazik?
rows.english: Hello, Mr. Horváth! Where are you traveling?
I'm going to Dunántúl, to Székesfehérvár.
Do you have a seat yet?
l'm looking for a vacant seat now.
Do you have a lot of luggage?

rows.hungarian: Ha utazom, soha sincs sok csomagom.
rows.english: I never have much luggage when I travel.

rows.hungarian: Csak ez az egy bőröndöm van.
rows.english: I have only this one suitcase.
I don't have much baggage either.

rows.hungarian: Igen. A feleségem sehova sem akar menni.
rows.english: Yes. My wife doesn't want to go anywhere.

rows.hungarian: Az én feleségem sem szeret utazni.
rows.english: My wife doesn't like to travel either.

rows.hungarian: De most néhány napra Siófokra megyünk.
rows.english: But now we're going for a few days to Siófok.

rows.hungarian: Jöjjön a mi fülkénkbe, még nincs ott senki sem.
rows.english: Come into our compartment nobody is there yet.

rows.hungarian: Tegye le a kabátját az ülésre.
rows.english: Put your coat down on the seat.
Mary, let me introduce Mr. Horváth.

rows.hungarian: Most megyünk át a vasúti hídon.
rows.english: We're now going over the railway bridge.

rows.hungarian: A folyosóról talán lehet látni a Margitszigetet.
rows.english: Perhaps we can see Margaret Island from the corridor.
Don't you want to come out?

rows.hungarian: Most megyünk át a Dunántúlra, nem?
rows.english: Now we're passing through to the Dunántúl, aren't we?

rows.hungarian: Igen. A Duna másik oldalán már a Dunántúl van.
rows.english: Yes. The other side of the Danube is already the Dunántúl.

rows.hungarian: Azért nevezik ezt a vidéket Dunántúlnak, mert a Dunán túl van.
rows.english: They call this region Dunántúl because it's beyond the Danube.
Do you know the Dunántúl well?

rows.hungarian: Igen. Van egy kis gyümölcsösöm Székesfehérvar mellett.
rows.english: Yes. I have a small orchard near Székesfehérvár.
And I go to the Balaton to spend my summer vacation.

rows.hungarian: Az idén mi is a Balatonon nyaralunk.
rows.english: This year we're going to spend our summer vacation also at the Balaton.

rows.hungarian: Nézze, milyen szép az a kukoricaföld a tanya mellett!
rows.english: Look how beautiful that cornfield next to the farm is!
Yes, the land is good there.

rows.hungarian: De a másik oldalon nem termelnek semmit sem.
rows.english: But on the other side they don't grow anything at all.

rows.hungarian: Érdekes, nem látok senkit sem dolgozni a földeken.
rows.english: It's interesting but I don't see anyone at all working in the fields.

rows.hungarian: Ma ünnep van, nem dolgoznak sehol sem.
rows.english: Since today is a holiday, nobody is working anywhere.

rows.hungarian: Nézze, mennyi gyerek játszik az úton!
rows.english: Look how many children there are playing on the road!

rows.hungarian: Egy gyerek éppen most mászik le a fárol es felül a kerékpárjára.
rows.english: One child is just climbing down from the tree and getting on his bicycle.

rows.hungarian: De megkérdezem a kalauztól, hogy van-e étkezőkocsi a vonaton.
rows.english: But I'll ask the conductor if there's a dining car on the train.

rows.hungarian: A mi kocsink után a második az étkezőkocsi.
rows.english: It's the second after our car.

rows.hungarian: Nem tudtam, hogy már Budapesten vannak.
rows.english: I didn't know that you were in Budapest already.
the first (with reference to the day of the month)
We got back (already) three weeks ago.
We arrived in Budapest on April first.
Did you have a pleasant trip?

rows.hungarian: Csak szegény kislányom az egész idő alatt beteg volt.
rows.english: Only my poor daughter was sick alI the time.

rows.hungarian: Igen, pedig a tenger elég nyugodt volt.
rows.english: Yes, although the sea was quite calm.
How long did the voyage last?
We spent a week in London.

rows.hungarian: A bátyám is velünk jött Angliáig.
rows.english: My older brother carne as far as England with us also.
What did you do in America?
That would take long to tell now.

rows.hungarian: Miért nem jön át egyik este hozzánk?
rows.english: Why don't you come over some evening?

rows.hungarian: Még mindig a régi lakásukban laknak?
rows.english: Are you still living in your old apartment?

rows.hungarian: Hallottam, hogy kerestek egy másik lakást.
rows.english: I heard that you had been looking for another apartment.

rows.hungarian: Két hete elköltöztünk a Városmajor utcába.
rows.english: We moved to Városmajor Street two weeks ago.
We live in a beautiful villa there.

rows.hungarian: Smithék is a városmajor utcában laknak, nem?
rows.english: The Smiths are also living on Városmajor Street, aren't they?

rows.hungarian: Mi abba a házba költöztünk, ahol ők laktak.
rows.english: We moved into the house where they used to live.
How long was Smith in Hungary?
He was here for three years.
And how long are you going to stay in Pest?

rows.hungarian: Azt hiszem, Ember János ül ott a sarokban.
rows.english: I believe that's John Ember sitting over there in the corner.
I'm going over to his table.
I haven' t talked to him for a very long time.

rows.hungarian: A feleségem már tíz perce vár.
rows.english: My wife has already been waiting for me for ten minutes.
We're going to Buda to the Kovácses.

rows.hungarian: Tegnap este láttam magukat az operában.
rows.english: I saw you at the opera last night.
I'm sorry that I didn't see you.
Why didn't you come over and talk to us?
I didn't want to disturb you.
You were sitting in the Ambassador's box, weren't you?

rows.hungarian: Igen, neki bérlete van és meghívott minket.
rows.english: Yes, he has a season ticket and invited us.

rows.hungarian: Én másfél óráig álltam sorba a jegyért.
rows.english: I had to stand in line an hour and a half for the ticket.

rows.hungarian: Amikor külföldi operaénekesek szerepelnek, mindig nehéz jegyet kapni.
rows.english: When foreign opera singers are (perform) on the program it's always hard to get a ticket.
But it was worth it, wasn't it?

rows.hungarian: Igen. Nekem nagyon tetszett az előadás.
rows.english: Yes. liked the performance very much.

rows.hungarian: Én is szeretem az olaszokat és az olasz operákat.
rows.english: Like Italians and Italian operas also.

rows.hungarian: Két évvel ezelőtt Rómában dolgoztam a követségen.
rows.english: Two years aga I worked at the embassy in Rome.

rows.hungarian: Akkor volt alkalmam majdnem minden olasz operát megnézni.
rows.english: Then I had the opportunity to see just about all the Italian operas.
up to the present, till now
till then, as far as that
Up to now I have seen only a few.

rows.hungarian: Hallotta, hogya nyáron a Bécsi Filharmónikusok jönnek Pestre?
rows.english: Did you hear that the Vienna Philharmonic is coming to Pest this summer?

rows.hungarian: A múlt héten olvastam az újságban.
rows.english: I read about it in the newspaper last week.
Are you going to the concert?
Of course, if I can get a ticket.

rows.hungarian: Mi már telefonáltunk a követségről a jegyirodába.
rows.english: We already phoned the ticket office from the Legation.
They promised us that we'd get enough tickets.

rows.hungarian: Talán tudok magának is szerezni egyet.
rows.english: Perhaps I can get you one also.
I'll be very grateful if you do.

rows.hungarian: Bécsben nem tudtam menni, mert nem volt elég időm.
rows.english: I couldn't go in Vienna because I didn't have enough time.
I heard that you had been working hard in Vienna.
But this will be a good opportunity.
Thank you very much in advance.
Don't mention it. It's nothing at all.

rows.hungarian: Ott volt azon a sajtóértekezleten, amit a miniszterelnök tartott?
rows.english: Were you at the press conference that the Prime Minister held?
I'm busy, I have something to do

rows.hungarian: Nem tudtam elmenni, mert más dolgom volt.
rows.english: I couldn't go because I had some other business.

rows.hungarian: Majdnem minden követségről ott volt a kereskedelmi attasé.
rows.english: The commercial attachés of almost all the legations were there.

rows.hungarian: A külkereskedelmi minisztériumból nem volt senki?
rows.english: Wasn't there anyone from the Ministry of Foreign Trade?

rows.hungarian: De igen. A Kormány, a párt és a sajto képviselői mindig ott vannak.
rows.english: Oh, yes. The representatives of the government, party and press are always present.
What did the Prime Minister talk about?

rows.hungarian: A termelésről, a munkaversenyről és az életszínvonal emeléséről.
rows.english: The production, work competition and raising of living standards.

rows.hungarian: Beszélt arról, hogy milyen magas lesz az életszínvonal öt év mulva.
rows.english: He spoke about how high the living standard will be after five years.
We've already heard that many times.

rows.hungarian: Igen. Elmondta, hogy nemrég kötöttek egy új kereskedelmi szerződest a Szovjetunióval.
rows.english: Yes. He told us that they have recently concluded a new commercial treaty with the Soviet Union.

rows.hungarian: Hol irták alá a szerződést? Moszkvában?
rows.english: Where did they sign the treaty? In Moscow?
As far as I know, in Budapest.

rows.hungarian: Ez már a tizenhatodik szerződés Magyarország és a Szovjetunió között.
rows.english: This is already the 16th treaty between Hungary and the Soviet Union.
When did they make the first one?
After the war, on the 7th of November, 1945.

rows.hungarian: Nem tudtam, hogy szovjet bizottság jött Budapestre.
rows.english: I didn't know that a Soviet delegation had come to Budapest.

rows.hungarian: Az újságok két héttel ezelőtt írtak róla.
rows.english: The newspapers wrote about it two weeks ago.

rows.hungarian: Nem emlékszik a cikkre a Népszabadságban?
rows.english: Don't you remember the article in the Népszabadság?
It was on the first page.

rows.hungarian: Nem tudtam, hogy erről a bizottságról írtak.
rows.english: I didn't know that they had written about this delegation.

rows.hungarian: És mi van az új szerződésben?
rows.english: And what's in the new treaty?

rows.hungarian: A szerződés szerint Magyarország növeli a különböző áruk szállítását.
rows.english: According to the treaty Hungary is increasing its shipments of various types of merchandise.

rows.hungarian: Ez azt jelenti, hogy Magyarország ezután többet szállít, mint eddig?
rows.english: Does this mean that from now on Hungary is going to transport more than it has up to the present time?

rows.hungarian: Igen. És van ennek a szerződésnek egy érdekes pontja.
rows.english: Yes. And this treaty has an interesting clause.
The times of delivery are short.

rows.hungarian: És mit küld cserébe a Szovjetunió?
rows.english: And what is the Soviet Union sending in exchange?
It's sending the Moiseyev dance group.

rows.hungarian: De mondja, mit mondott még a miniszterelnök?
rows.english: But tell me, what else did the Prime Minister say?

rows.hungarian: Beszélt az ötéves tervről meg a termelésről.
rows.english: He was talking about the five year plan and about production.

rows.hungarian: A jövő évben ketszer annyi gabonát kell termelni, mint a múlt évben.
rows.english: Next year we have to grow twice the quantity of crops as last year.

rows.hungarian: Ebben az évben nem volt jó a termés.
rows.english: This year the harvest hasn't been good.
They hope next year it'll be good.
When will the next press conference take place?
It'll be two weeks from now, on the 24th.
Do you know where they're holding it?

rows.hungarian: Nem tudom pontosan, de a pénzügyminiszter helyettese tartja.
rows.english: I don't know exactly, but the Deputy Minister of Finance is holding it.
Do you know the Deputy Minister of Finance?
He's a pleasant and smart man.

rows.hungarian: Erre az értekezletre én is elmegyek, ha tudok.
rows.english: I'm going to this conference if I can.

rows.hungarian: Sokszor meg akartam hallgatni Kertész urat, de sohasem volt alkalmam.
rows.english: I have often wanted to hear Mr. Kertész but have never had the opportunity.
What's he going to talk about?

rows.hungarian: A gazdasági életről és a drágaság okairól.
rows.english: About the economic life and the reasons for the high cost of living.

rows.hungarian: Meg akarja magyarázni, hogy miért nincs elég áru az országban.
rows.english: He wants to explain why we don't have enough merchandi'se in the country.
I believe you're familiar with this problem.

rows.hungarian: Oh igen, sokszor próbálták már megmagyarázni.
rows.english: Why, yes, they've tried to explain it many times.

rows.hungarian: De a tény az, hogya munkás nem tud eleget keresni.
rows.english: But the fact is that the workers can't make enough money.

rows.hungarian: Gondolja, hogy valaki megkérdezi, hogy mit akar tenni a kormány?
rows.english: Do you think anybody will ask what the government intends to do?

rows.hungarian: Én nem kérdezem meg, de valaki biztosan megkéri Kertész urat, hogy beszéljen erről a kérdésről is.
rows.english: I'm not going to ask, but somebody will surely ask Mr. Kertész to talk about this question also.

rows.hungarian: Remélem, talál a kormány megoldást ezekre a gazdasági kérdésekre.
rows.english: I hope the government can find a solution to these economic problems.

rows.hungarian: Meg akarom kérni, hogy vigyen el 24-én a kocsiján.
rows.english: I want to ask you to take me in your car on the 24th.

rows.hungarian: Az én kocsirnat a feleségem használja.

rows.english: to find out, get to know.

rows.hungarian: Addig megtudom pontosan, hol lesz az értekezlet.
rows.english: In the meantime I'll find out exactly where the meeting will be.
